channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed

item.title: Square Enix Announces Final Fantasy XIV Online's Next Expansion 'Evercold'
item.content:encoded:
And reveals a legendary collab

Apart from the news Final Fantasy XIV Online is on its way to the Switch 2 this August, Square Enix also announced Evercold.

This is the sixth expansion for the popular MMORPG and was revealed during Naoki Yoshida's keynote address at FFXIV Fan Festival 2026. It's currently set to launch in January 2027 on PC, Mac, Steam, PlayStation and Xbox platforms.

The description attached to the video above also confirms it's "coming to" the Nintendo Switch 2.

This same trailer kicks off the Godless Realms Saga story arc and offers a glimpse into the adventures the Warrior of Light will experience on the fourth. Here's what you can expect from this upcoming expansion and you can find out more on the official game site.

  • Two new jobs: tank and physical ranged DPS
  • Level cap increase from 100 to 110
  • New cities
  • Distinctive new areas
  • New gigantic constructs and other monstrous threats to overcome
  • New allies to encounter
  • New dungeons
  • New trials
  • A new raid series
  • A new Ultimate raid
  • PvP updates
  • Ongoing content updates, including new gear and crafting recipes, updates to the Duty Support System and more

During the same reveal, Yoshi-P also announced an "unprecedented" battle system overhaul, with Reborn and Evolved modes coming to FFXIV Online alongside the launch of Evercold.

"Reborn Mode will be based on the current combat system and job mechanics, while Evolved Mode offers greater emphasis on job identity."

Additionally, there'll be expanded updates to the game's design, including a seasons overhaul (updating how players earn rewards and improve their characters), an armoury update, and expanded character customisation.

Square Enix has also revealed XIV is crossing over with the Japanese anime Evangelion. Here's a bit about it and what you can expect from it:

"The exciting new alliance raid series is a crossover with the hugely popular Japanese anime, EVANGELION, and will be created in collaboration with khara, Inc., best known for its work on the EVANGELION series. Since EVANGELION’s first television broadcast in 1995, the anime’s mysterious and profound storyline, alongside its innovative and stylish visual expression, has had a profound influence on numerous creators around the world, with the iconic franchise later expanding its library with multiple animated films and a comic adaptation."

What do you think of the latest expansion reveal for Final Fantasy XIV Online? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/04/square-enix-announces-final-fantasy-xiv-onlines-next-expansion-evercold
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Poll: So, Will You Be Playing Final Fantasy XIV Online On Switch 2?
item.content:encoded:
Go on, tell us

After ongoing discussions about bringing the popular MMORPG Final Fantasy XIV Online to Nintendo's platform, it's finally been revealed at this week's Final Fantasy XIV Fan Festival.

Final Fantasy XIV Online will be arriving in August 2026 on the Switch 2. As we've already noted, players will be able to get started with one-month period of early access. After this, you'll need to purchase a separate subscription for this version of the game.

If you already have a subscription on another platform, the Switch 2 subscription can be purchased at half-price. It's also been confirmed a Switch Online subscription won't be required to play on Switch 2. You can find out more about this version in the announcement post and Square Enix's website.

So, will you be giving Final Fantasy XIV Online a go on Switch 2? Vote in our poll and let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/04/poll-so-will-you-be-playing-final-fantasy-xiv-online-on-switch-2
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Star Wars: Galactic Racer Release Date Seemingly Leaked on Steam
item.content:encoded:
Star Wars: Galactic Racer, the upcoming arcade racing game featuring podracers, speeder bikes, landspeeders, and more fast rides from the Star Wars universe will apparently be released on October 6. The release date was posted to Steam and quickly removed, but not before eagle-eyed storefront watcher Wario64 spotted it.

"STAR WARS: Galactic Racer will release on Oct 6, 2026 according to Steam. Deluxe Edition contents revealed (Steelbook included with physical purchase)," Wario64 noted in their post. A check of the Galactic Racer Steam page an hour later showed a generic "2026" release date.

Galactic Racer was announced at the 2025 Game Awards, and it's being developed by Fuse Games, a team comprised of a number of former Burnout and Need for Speed developers founded in 2023. It was last seen at Sony's State of Play broadcast back in February.

Regardless of whether or not the October 6 release date holds, we've still got new Star Wars to look forward to in the near future, with The Mandalorian and Grogu set to hit theaters next month. We got the final trailer earlier this week.

Ryan McCaffrey is IGN's executive editor of previews and host of both IGN's weekly Xbox show, Podcast Unlocked, as well as our semi-retired interview show, IGN Unfiltered. He's a North Jersey guy, so it's "Taylor ham," not "pork roll." Debate it with him on Twitter at @DMC_Ryan.



https://www.ign.com/articles/star-wars-galactic-racer-release-date-leaked-steam
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Marvel’s Venom Game Canceled Following Passing of Tony Todd, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
The voice actor for Miles Morales in Insomniac’s Spider-Man video games has provided insight into why the rumored Venom title will not be released.

Following Venom’s appearance in Spider-Man 2 for the PlayStation 5, speculation arose about a Venom-focused project. However, according to Nadji Jeter, the voice actor for Miles Morales, on the Love It Film podcast, the game was canceled after Tony Todd, the voice actor for Venom, passed away in 2024.

“Let me give y’all an exclusive. We were going to have a Venom game. We were going to drop a Venom game and a Venom DLC, but we lost Tony Todd,” he said. “They showed me how everything was going to be. It was gonna be a Venom video game. Y’all played the Venom playthrough? It was just gonna be a Venom video game.”

Conflicting Reports Regarding Marvel’s Venom Title

In response to Jeter’s claims, Jason Schreier of Bloomberg commented on the Reset Era forums that this wasn’t true. In the same form discussion, a verified developer claimed the game was still being discussed at this year’s GDC.

The plans for the game would have made it a half-sequel, in the same way that Spider-Man: Miles Morales bridges the gap between the first Insomniac Spider-Man game and the sequel. While the Venom project appears to have been canceled, Insomniac is full steam ahead with the upcoming Wolverine game.

The upcoming title is slated for release in September and will carry an M rating, allowing for all the violent Logan action. Learn more about the game here.

In September of last year, it was reported that the Venom project would center on Eddie Brock. It was also reported that anti-Venom would be a part of the game, and it would have nothing to do with the Venom revealed in the second Spider-Man title. It was also reportedly slated to come after the Wolverine game.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor everything regarding the Venom project and report any updates to the story.

How do you feel about the supposedly canceled Venom project? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/marvels-venom-game-canceled-following-passing-of-tony-todd-its-claimed/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: People Can Fly acquires Cooldown Games
item.content:encoded:
People Can Fly to establish a dedicated publishing vertical within the group.

People Can Fly has acquired the Cooldown Games brand and its publishing rights, and will establish a dedicated publishing vertical within the group’s operating structure, the company announced. The acquisition advances People Can Fly’s strategy to strengthen its self-publishing activity.

Under People Can Fly, Cooldown Games will operate as an independent publishing division. The existing Cooldown Games leadership team will remain in place to lead the division.

People Can Fly will leverage Cooldown Games’ existing infrastructure, platform relationships, and commercial expertise. The publishing division will provide targeted support to internally developed titles, as well as pursue third-party publishing agreements.

“Although we are strongly focused on the work-for-hire segment to achieve positive cash flows across the Group, we have never intended to abandon our publishing plans,” said People Can Fly Group CEO Sebastian Wojciechowski in a press release. “Rather, we have been waiting for the right moment for them to regain traction and scale. While this is happening somewhat earlier than we originally anticipated, it is based on two key pillars: the trust in the Cooldown Games team built over the years, and an approach in which the publishing segment is intended to be revenue-generating from the outset, rather than cost-intensive.”

Cooldown Games CEO Steve Gibson added, “We’ve built Cooldown Games around championing exceptional developers and bringing their games to players around the world. Joining People Can Fly gives us the scale, resources, and long-term stability to do that at an even higher level. We know firsthand the great work that this team is capable of, and we’re excited to build the next generation of great games together.”

According to People Can Fly, the acquisition enables it to:

  • Generate recurring revenue via third-party publishing agreements.
  • Recognize increased profit margins on internally developed titles.
  • Improve capital allocation discipline through integrated greenlight oversight.
  • Enhance strategic flexibility for future M&A or IP monetization initiatives.

“Rebuilding our publishing capabilities is an important step in People Can Fly’s continued evolution,” added Wojciechowski. “Bringing the Cooldown Games team into the organization allows us to build on the publishing foundation we’ve been developing for years while strengthening our ability to take great games to market globally. This structure strengthens our control over commercial outcomes and positions us to capture greater value across the lifecycle of our titles, addressing challenges we have faced in the past.”



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/people-can-fly-acquires-cooldown-games
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Star Wars Galactic Racer Release Date Leaked
item.content:encoded:
The upcoming Star Wars racing game, Star Wars: Galactic Racer, has new details confirmed on its official Steam page.

The game, which was revealed at the 2025 Game Awards, is officially coming on October 6, 2026, according to a new graphic showcasing the pre-order bonuses. Those who pre-order the title will receive a bonus Livery for their Repulsorcraft and an exclusive player Banner that can be used in the multiplayer modes.

That’s just for those who pre-order the standard edition of the game. Depending on how much money you’re willing to spend, there are even more goodies.

Star Wars: Galactic Racer Deluxe Edition Description

The Steam page has also revealed what’s in store for those who pre-order the game’s deluxe edition. In addition to the pre-order bonuses mentioned above, there are also exclusive Vehicles, a digital art book, three exclusive arcade events, and more.

Check out the full write-up below:

​​​The Star Wars: Galactic Racer™ Deluxe Edition features: 

  • Three exclusive vehicles: Become the envy of the paddock and pilot the Kor Sarun: Ciza T speeder bike, the Kor Sarun: Darc X landspeeder, and the Kor Sarun: Rak S skim speeder 
  • Unique Livery for pilots who wish to race in style. Coat any of your speeders with an exclusive Livery inspired by the Naboo N-1 starfighter 
  • Three Arcade Events for the fearless racers. The Galactic League challenges you to three exclusive Arcade trials designed specifically for your Kor Sarun repulsorcraft 
  • Deluxe Player Banner containing an exclusive Insignia and Background for multiplayer modes 
  • Digital Art Book showcasing never-before-seen character, vehicle, and location artwork from the development of Star Wars: Galactic Racer™ 

As of yet, there are no official price points for either edition of the game. It was recently revealed that the game will launch with Nvidia DLSS 4.5. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available.

Are you looking forward to playing Star Wars: Galactic Racer? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/star-wars-galactic-racer-release-date-leaked/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Final Fantasy XIV Is Coming to Nintendo Switch 2 in August With a Separate Subscription
item.content:encoded:
Early access will be free the month before launch.

Square Enix has announced plans to bring Final Fantasy XIV to Nintendo Switch 2 this August – but there is a catch.

FFXIV director and producer Naoki Yoshida (a.k.a. Yoshi-P) and Square Enix president and CEO Takashi Kiryu revealed plans to bring the massively popular MMORPG to the Nintendo platform as part of Final Fantasy XIV Fan Fest 2026 today. It’s news that marked an end to a long wait, but it also came with the reveal that players on the hybrid gaming platform will need to rethink their subscription plans.

The presentation continued following the announcement with a graphic confirming that subscriptions for FFXIV on Switch 2 will operate differently than other platforms. While players previously only needed to purchase the game itself and the monthly subscription fee to play on any platform, the Switch 2 version will require players to purchase an additional, separate subscription.

"After discussions with Nintendo, it was decided that the Switch 2 version of Final Fantasy XIV would require a separate subscription," Yoshi-P said via translator. "Again, this was decided after many months of discussions with Nintendo, and I understand that this is different to how we’ve done things before."

Thankfully, Yoshi-P said that there were able to make some concessions. A Nintendo Switch Online membership, for example, will not be required to play FFXIV on Switch 2. Additionally, those with active subscriptions on other platforms will receive a 50% discount for a subscription on Switch 2.

Final Fantasy XIV currently has no specific release date for the Switch 2 version outside of the promise to see its launch arrive in August. It will be playable via early access to try for free for one month before its release. The Nintendo portion of the presentation then came to an end with a live demo of the game running on Switch 2.

"A week ago, I had this great idea to play the Switch 2 on stage here, and Nintendo kind of freaked out a little,” Yoshi-P added. "They were like, 'What?!' But they cooperated and helped us so much to make this happen, so thank you, Nintendo, very much to help us make this happen on-stage today."

The base game Final Fantasy XIV: A Realm Reborn originally launched in 2013 and, after only a few teases in the years since, it is finally coming to a Nintendo platform. Also revealed at the 2026 Fan Fest keynote, the upcoming expansion, Evercold, is coming in January 2027. and got an announcement trailer which you can see above. A crossover with Neon Genesis Evangelion is also in the works, which will be a 24-player raid series coming after Evercold.

Michael Cripe is a freelance writer with IGN. He's best known for his work at sites like The Pitch, The Escapist, and OnlySP. Be sure to give him a follow on Bluesky (@mikecripe.bsky.social) and Twitter (@MikeCripe).

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/final-fantasy-xiv-is-coming-to-nintendo-switch-2-in-august-with-a-separate-subscription
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Warframe Reveals Jade Shadows: Constellations With Intense New Animated Short & First Details
item.content:encoded:
Warframe’s mysterious “Unannounced 2026 update” is Jade Shadows: Constellations, a surprise sequel to 2024’s Jade Shadows. Digital Extremes revealed the update in the latest Devstream, along with an animated short made in collaboration with THE LINE and the metal band ERRA.

The new animated short reveals two rivals from two future timelines, clashing to the sounds of “Crawl Backwards Out of Heaven” by ERRA. There are two versions to watch, one centering each of the two characters, Orion and Sirius. These two rivals turn out to be (spoiler!) the Stalker’s children. Jade Shadows: Constellations puts you in control of the Stalker once more, this time as a father. You’ll have either Sirius or Orion based on choices you made in Jade Shadows, and watch the potential futures collide.

The new Narrative Chapter will expand greatly on this when it’s out in June. Also coming in the update are new Protoframes. Vena and Ryoku are the Protoframe versions of Garuda and Ash. Each will have been caring for one of the brothers and will play a big role in the events and conflict.

The Uranus-Proxima region of the Railjack Star Chart is going to open, revealing two new mission nodes connected to Vena and Ryoku. 

We’ll also see a Nidus retouch, with updates including boosted health and ability improvements. Also on the way? Dante Tytonis, the deluxe incarnation of Dante, in addition to a new skin for his Tome.

Returning much sooner is the Operation: Belly of the Beast, which has you help the Stalker defend the memory of Jade from Parvoa Granum. This will be back from May 4th to June 1st and will have new and returning rewards,  including some that you earned by playing with clanmates.

Expect a new Devstream in May for lots more on Jade Shadows: Constellations and more.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/warframe-reveals-jade-shadows-constellations-with-intense-new-animated-short-and-first-details-2000137899
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Neon Genesis Evangelion Is Coming to Final Fantasy XIV With a Crossover Raid Series Called Ghosts of Desire
item.content:encoded:
As part of the Final Fantasy XIV Fan Fest keynote presentation, director and producer Naoki Yoshida revealed that the upcoming 24-player raid series will be a crossover with the iconic anime series Neon Genesis Evangelion. It's called Ghosts of Desire and is part of the post-game content for the next expansion FFXIV Evercold, which launches in January 2027. You can see the FFXIV x Evangelion teaser below.

This crossover isn't as unlikely as you may think. The creative mind behind NieR Automata, Yoko Taro, worked with the FFXIV team for the Shadowbringers raid series that brought NieR-themed content to the MMORPG. But he's also doing the composition and screenplay for the new Evangelion anime series alongside Studio Khara (which is headed by Evangelion creator Hideaki Anno).

If you're not familiar, 24-player raids (called Alliance Raids) are endgame content for FFXIV and roll out through updates between expansions. If this collab follows the same cadence as previous patches, Ghosts of Desire would come in three parts with its own story arc through Patch 8.1, 8.3, and 8.5. It'll contain boss fights and dungeons themed around Evangelion, and will somehow exist in lore with FFXIV.

Fore more on what's coming to the critically acclaimed MMORPG, check out my roundup of announcements from Fan Fest including the reveal of FFXIV Evercold.



https://www.ign.com/articles/neon-genesis-evangelion-is-coming-to-final-fantasy-xiv-with-a-crossover-raid-series-called-ghosts-of-desire
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Official Baldur's Gate 3 Cookbook Sounds Like a Culinary Walkthrough of the Game
item.content:encoded:
The Official Baldur's Gate 3 Cookbook Sounds Like a Culinary Walkthrough of the Game

Another use for the owlbear egg.

A Feast for a Tenday: The Official Baldur’s Gate 3 Cookbook has been announced as one of four BG3-themed books releasing this year.

Written by Dungeons & Dragons veteran Andrew Wheeler, the cookbook is already up for preorder on Amazon with a release date of November 3. The hardcover edition is priced at $38, while a Kindle edition is down to $14.99 with a 60% discount.

A Feast for Tenday: The Official Baldur’s Gate 3 Cookbook Releasing November 3

A Feast for a Tenday (Baldur's Gate 3): The Official Baldur's Gate 3 Cookbook (Hardcover)

The BG3 cookbook includes 65 recipes inspired by the ingredients, locations, quests, and characters of Baldur’s Gate. The recipes are accompanied by 45 photographs as well as original illustrations, amounting to 208 pages in total.

Unlike most video game-inspired cookbooks, A Feast for a Tenday frames its recipes in the context of the game’s original story. Per the cookbook’s description: “Join the adventure as a cook traveling with Lae’zel, Shadowheart, Astarion, Gale, Wyll, and Karlach from the Wilderness, through the Underdark and the Shadow-Cursed Lands, all the way to Baldur’s Gate.” No love for the Mountain Pass, it seems.

Some of the recipes mentioned in Amazon’s listing include a scotched Owlbear Egg, Neverwinter Soup, and Bex’s Handmade Cookies. The cookbook also includes drink recipes like Frostkiss Ale and Turmishan Tea. Shareable recipes will be designated as “Romanceable,” and I wouldn’t be surprised if we see individual recipes tied to specific romance candidates.

The cookbook’s author, Andrew Wheeler, is a well-known writer in the world of Dungeons & Dragons. He wrote the Dungeons & Dragons cocktail book, Puncheons & Flagons, which offers a reasonable indication of what to expect from this new release. Wheeler has also written the Dungeon Master’s Workbook of Worldbuilding and a number of Young Adventurer’s Guides, so it’s safe to say he knows his D&D.

The cookbook joins a host of official Baldur’s Gate 3 books releasing over the next couple of months. Other highlights include a prequel novel written by the award-winning fantasy author T. Kingfisher about, you guessed it, Astarion. Clarkson Potter will also be publishing a hardcover Necromancy of Thay notebook in July.

In other BG3 news, HBO abruptly announced they were developing a Baldur’s Gate 3 TV series back in February. The news did not go over well with the original devs, who had not been involved in the announcement, leaving a bit of a question mark as to where the project stands now. In the meantime, Larian's focus is on Divinity, a new turn-based RPG that certainly has some high expectations attached to it.

Blythe (she/her) is an Audience Development Coordinator at IGN who spends way too much time in character customization screens and tracking down collectibles.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/a-feast-for-a-tenday-official-baldurs-gate-3-cookbook-where-to-buy
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Final Fantasy XIV's Next Expansion, Evercold, Takes Players To The Fourth - Here Are All The Details From Today's Keynote
item.content:encoded:
During Final Fantasy XIV Fan Fest in Anaheim, California, Square Enix officially revealed Final Fantasy XIV - Evercold, the next expansion launching January 2027 for all platforms. The Japanese title roughly translates to “Wanderer (or Explorer) of the Silver.” There was a metric ton of details shared on both Evercold and some massive foundation-changing updates for FFXIV at large. We’ll cover the big highlights here.

Evercold Details

This expansion moves FFXIV into what Square Enix says is the next major story arc for the MMORPG, called the “Godless Realms Saga.” The teaser trailer is framed as a bedtime story for an Au’ra child, taking place on a frozen planet where the Warrior of Light, Alphinaud, and Estinien come through a portal – which director Naoki Yoshida confirmed is the world of the Fourth Reflection. This reflection is slowly being ravaged by ever-spreading ice.

Yoshida kept story details vague, but did say that the upcoming Patch 7.5 will directly set up Evercold. During the keynote Yoshida shared a number of screenshots for new areas from the expansion, and while we didn’t get any names or major details, there’s a clear sort of high-fantasty, or almost Norse Mythology inspiration. Equally interesting is the fact that despite the expansion being called Evercold, many of the environments shown off were heavily mechanical and filled with lava and fire – suggesting something more is happening. 

While showing off some of Evercold’s town area concept art, Yoshida noted the style is “fantastical,” and that’s a clear indication of the art direction this expansion is taking. There were heavily gilded residential areas filled with spires, multicolored flower gardens, and seaside fishing shacks. 

The keynote also shared a look at a delightful new race introduced with Evercold, squat little cat-people that look a lot like dwarves. Unfortunately, we didn’t learn any more about them quite yet. 

Evercold will feature all of the normal expansion additions, including raising the level cap to 110, new dungeons, a new 8-player raid, new gear and recipes, and more.

There will be a new Alliance Raid called “Ghosts of Desire,” themed around Neon Genesis Evangelion, in collaboration for the series’ 30th anniversary. Square Enix will be developing the raid content, while the producer of Evangelion, Khara, will be supervising. 

Game System Changes

After a brief look at narrative and visual elements, Yoshida covered some of the game design updates coming with 8.0. Yoshida stressed that the last 13 years of FFXIV have allowed the game to grow to this point, but the development team wants to go a direction with Evercold – an emphasis on the game “growing” and “evolving.”

Yoshida emphasized this by showing off a completely new and rebooted Adventurer Activity screen. The game will move from Daily-based systems to Weekly-based systems, specifically for the bonuses attached to tomestones. This is an effort to combat the “weariness” that some players have with the daily systems, hoping to better fit the playstyles and lifestyles of FFXIV players. There will now be weekly missions that reward Activity Points. As you earn Activity Points you’ll raise your Activity Level, which gives you gear and other rewards. Some missions can only be completed once, while others are repeatable. Yoshida notes that the system is not fixed in any way, and will likely be adjusted over time. 

There are changes for item level progression as well, with new ways to raise item level. However, we won’t learn those details until Fan Fest Tokyo. 

The next addition is Seasons, but Yoshida stressed this does not mean extra fees or the typical idea of a Season Pass. FFXIV’s Seasons equals 2 patches (Even+Odd), for example 8.0 and 8.1 will be one “Season.” This will hopefully enable the development team to shorten the time between patches by sort of bundling them together. 

Tne of the focal points of Evercold’s game design is leaning into making each and every player’s character different. This starts with an update to the Armoury System, something that has barely been changed in over a decade. In 8.0 jobs will still exist and be leveled separately, but gear will reflect your highest item level in your Armoury, irrespective of job. This will allow more freedom in how players can approach content and playing as jobs, such as being able to take a job into a Savage Raid that you may not have the right gear for. More details on this system will be revealed in the future. 

The other major addition is Character Action Skins, letting you customize your characters with specific actions – giving you more freedom to set apart your avatar. More details on these skins will be revealed at Fan Fest Berlin. 

Character creation options will be improved and broadened, increasing the options you have for customizing your avatar. These include a color picker for hair, eyes, and skin tone, face paint layering, and improved customization of eyes and lips. You’ll even be able to toggle on and off certain pieces of gear, allowing for more outfit variety. A few comparison screenshots were shown off. All of the options shown off at Anaheim are planned for the 8.0 release, but additional updates will come later on. 

The next topic covered was what Square Enix is calling “Improving Public Fields.” To do this, more dynamic events and content will be added to field areas. Alongside this, the development team is looking to improve how quests are played – quality of life features that will “ease the burden” of questing. Again, this is a topic that will be explored more at Fan Fest Berlin. 

Battle content will be enhanced in three key ways

  • Adding a third difficulty level for 8-person raids (More details at Berlin)
  • Adjusting progression routes for 24-persona raids (More details at Japan)
  • All-new types of battle content

The final Game Design update is same-region player matching, meaning player matching will include all worlds across an entire region – a much-requested fan feature. This includes Party Finder. These updates will be coming with the Patch 7.5x series, with first tests happening in Japan before expanding to the US, EU, and China. 

Job Changes

In terms of jobs, Yoshida confirmed Evercold will have two new jobs, a Tank and Ranged DPS. Both jobs are entirely original to FFXIV, instead of based on legacy jobs from past Final Fantasy games. 

But the bigger news is a change to the job action system, as the game will now have a Reborn Mode and Evolved Mode. These two modes will only be available for the 21 main jobs, and not the limited jobs of Blue Mage and Beastmaster. 

Reborn Mode is a familiar system, essentially the job action and rotations that players have been using since A Realm Reborn. It’ll exist for all 21 current jobs. Reborn is what players know, but like always will be updated with new expansions and patches. 

Meanwhile, Evolved Mode will also be available for all 21 jobs, and emphasizes individuality with different rotations. All jobs will have new action rotations different from the Reborn Mode, with fewer inputs. Yoshida says Evolved Mode is not a “watered down” or simplified version of the game, instead focused on giving the game a modern feel. Yoshida stresses that these two modes have different actions, practically like two unique jobs. Job level and gear is shared and you can toggle between the modes at any time outside of battle. All game content will be clearable with both modes. 

Most importantly, however, all jobs introduced from 8.0 and on will only have Evolved Mode. 

PS4 Version Update

Yoshida announced that FFXIV is nearing a breaking point for support on PS4, as the game is simply becoming too large for the system. The development team estimates that things should stay the same until roughly 8.3, but eventually PS4 won’t be supported anymore

Free Trial Update

With version 8.0 the free trial will now include everything up through Shadowbringers. 

Nintendo Switch 2 Version

At the end of the keynote, Square Enix president Takashi Kiryu announced that Final Fantasy XIV is coming to Nintendo Switch 2. The version will a launch this August, with an Early Access period of one month sometime before that. 

The Nintendo Switch 2 version will require a separate subscription from other version of the game. But a Nintendo Switch Online membership will not be required. 

Find out more on Evercold at the expansion minisite.

Hayes Madsen

A connoisseur of all things RPG related, and always looks for the deeper meaning in gaming. His love of Gundam is only matched by his love of Pizza.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/final-fantasy-xivs-next-expansion-evercold-takes-players-to-the-fourth-here-are-all-the-details-from-todays-keynote-2000137898
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Former Call of Duty Dev Has a Lead Role on Tarkov Head’s New Game
item.content:encoded:
Rant Gaming Studios, the developer behind Fragmentary Order, has revealed that Aaron Beck, former Call of Duty concept artist, is leading the conceptual work on the all-new shooter. Fragmentary Order is another ideation from the mind of Nikita Buyanov, the face of Escape from Tarkov, the ten-year-old extraction shooter that defined the entire genre.

In a post on social media, it was explained that Beck has worked with Rant Gaming on Fragmentary Order for more than a year and a half. Fans of Beck’s work have already spotted some of his trademark stylisation in Frago’s earliest content.

Rant Gaming Studios is ‘Cooking Hard’ on Frago

Fragmentary Order was only revealed a few weeks ago, but it has already grabbed the extraction market by the shoulders and given it a shake. That’s interesting, because it’s not an extraction title. But, with the clearest of ties to Nikita Buyanov and everything he has created over the years, it’s easy to imagine Escape from Tarkov’s fanbase crossing over into Fragmentary Order.

In a post on social media, Buyanov revealed that Aaron Beck, a former Call of Duty concept artist, has been fleshing out Frago’s style for many months already:

Beck is best known for leading the conceptual creation of assets in games like Call of Duty: Infinite Warfare, Modern Warfare, and Modern Warfare II. He also contributed to Black Ops III and Warzone’s 2.0 shift in 2022.

Once upon a time, Beck worked with Weta Workshop, a prestigious production studio, and worked on the likes of Avatar, Elysium, and Mad Max.

Even the quickest glance through Beck’s portfolio reveals a passion for mechanical creations, robotics, mechanized suits, and exoskeletons, almost all of which are front and centre in Fragmentary Order.

In a follow-up post, the Fragmentary Order team wrote:

Aaron is our Lead Concept Artist and cooking hard with this one, we are planning to share some in game concepts in nearest future.

Fans were quick to notice that in the CORIE ARG that was used to tease Fragmentary Order, a soldier called Sergeant Beck was mentioned in some of the game’s documents and lore. They’ve theorized that this is a nod to the artist.

Are you happy to hear that an artist with serious chops is aligned with Fragmentary Order? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that a Battlefield movie is being developed

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/former-cod-dev-tarkov-head-fragmentary-order-game/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Hell Clock coming to PS5, Xbox Series on June 2 alongside ‘Cursed War’ expansion
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Currently available for PC.

Publisher Mad Mushroom and developer Rogue Snail will release roguelike action RPG Hell Clock for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series on June 2 alongside the game’s first major expansion, “Cursed War,” the companies announced.

Hell Clock first launched for PC via Steam on June 18, 2025.

“The response to Hell Clock from [action] RPG and roguelike fans since launching last year has driven our team to continue building on Pajeu’s story, and we can’t wait to hear what console players think,” said Rogue Snail CEO Mark Venturelli in a press release. “In the ‘Cursed War’ expansion we’re adding more content, deeper systems, and uncovering more of our dark fantasy version of Brazilian history for everyone to dig into.”

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About the Base Game

Hell Clock is a combination of action RPG depth and roguelike speed, and a dark retelling of Brazil’s late 19th century War of Canudos. Play as Pajeu and descend into Purgatory and Hell, equipping Blessed Gear, Unique Relics, and powerful Skills in a fight against the demons of Brazil’s past, present, and future. The magical Hell Clock ticks, restricting how much time Pajeu can spend in the depths and reviving him each time he falls.

About the Expansion “Cursed War”

The “Cursed War” expansion explores another chapter in Brazilian history: the Paraguayan War, a conflict between Paraguay and the Triple Alliance of Brazil, Argentina, and Uruguay fought from 1864 to 1870. Relive Pajeu’s fateful past as a soldier, before the events of the base game and his return to Canudos.

  • New Story Act – Pajeu’s campaign in Paraguay adds an all-new Act to the Hell Clock campaign.
  • New Maps and Biomes to Explore – Fight through over 10 new environments, including Swamp, Blood River, and Campo Grande biomes.
  • More Powerful Builds – A slew of new Skills to create devastating builds around, and dozens of new Relics to optimize them.
  • Mettle-Testing Boss Fights – New areas include new bosses to take down, some of the most difficult yet faced in Hell Clock.
  • New Endgame Systems – Extend the adventure even further with new Endgame Systems to truly test your build: Endless Nightmares and the full version of Ascension.
  • Another Chance to Rewrite HistoryMore Brazilian History – Cursed War continues Hell Clock‘s tradition of exploring one of the darker times in real Brazilian history, with a prequel story around Pajeu’s part in a dark fantasy version of the Paraguayan War.

Watch a new trailer below.

Consoles and “Cursed War” Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/hell-clock-coming-to-ps5-xbox-series-on-june-2-alongside-cursed-war-expansion
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Don't Lose Aggro Review
item.content:encoded:
Making healers look good since 2001

Don’t Lose Aggro is a roguelite dungeon crawler inspired by MMORPG raiding. Developed in Unity, thegame is in Early Access, and is the passion project of 15-year tanking veteran Oren Koren. While thegameplay loop is somewhat unique, Don’t Lose Aggro is still very rough around the edges, but hopefullyKoren can continue and improve on the initial design.

Solo Tanking

The meat of Don’t Lose Aggro consists of escorting an NPC ally through a series of dungeons, wavedefense, or boss rush scenarios. At the moment, there’s only a brief overview of how to play and wherethe upgrade locations are without a story or any real objectives.

Currently, you can choose between a Healer or Ranger, and eventually there will be a summoner class.The Healer will periodically heal the player and has an active ability that does area-of-effect damage.The Ranger is supposed to shoot arrows when you gain aggro and has an active ability that makes themexplode and heal you, but I couldn’t get the Ranger to do much of anything besides teleport around theroom. The player can unlock 3 sets of tanking gear that dictate the default ability and somewhatinfluence the gameplay through passive abilities.

The default set of gear is the Crusader specialization with a whirlwind area attack that takes less damagebased on how many resources you currently have. The next spec is Disc has an active attack that willalso trigger whenever you block an attack and has a reduced cooldown on shield throw. The final is ViceGrip, which doesn’t have an auto attack but instead does increasing passive damage while holdingaggro. I found the third class to be kind of clunky and slower to kill than the other two, but I might justbe missing something.

The gameplay itself feels like a less-refined version of early World of Warcraft. It’s tab-target with anautomatic auto-attack and has a couple of options between the 5 active abilities. Initially, you’ll start offwith a single, damage-focused ability and unlock the rest as you level up during a playthrough.This is something that deviates from the typically tanking experience, as you’d normally enter a dungeonor raid with all your skills unlocked. However, since this is a roguelite, it makes more sense in thiscontext and it doesn’t take long to progress through the early levels and unlock the base version ofthese skills.

The skills include a gap-closer, main damage source, taunt, utility and crowd control.While there is some nuance to the choices, overall player skill seems to make more of a difference thanthe abilities themselves. For example, the Q ability can be a targeted charge or a forward leap, which ishonestly very difficult to aim effectively. There’s also an option between an area or single-target taunt,but the single-target can be spammed more frequently and seems to last longer.

After the first couple of levels, skill choices become options for upgrades, such as providing damagebuffs or blocking/dodging the next series of attacks. Other options seem more impactful, such as makingyour pull ability now taunt everything it hits, which basically makes it an even better taunt.

Rinse and Repeat

Unfortunately, at the moment there isn’t a whole lot of content available in Don’t Lose Aggro. For playercustomization, there are the three tanking classes and two allies as previously mentioned. The actualtanking content includes a single dungeon with two difficulty levels, three levels of wave defense andtwo boss rush stages.

Additionally, as players level up by progressing through dungeon runs or clearing waves/bosses, they’llunlock perks for the skill trees. There are currently 3 skill trees: Offense, Defense, and Support. Theirdescriptions are pretty straight forward. Either do more damage, take less damage, or buff your allyand/or indirectly improve yourself.

I went with the Support tree first, which may have been a mistake since there doesn’t seem to be a wayto reset your skills points, but during my first few runs my ally kept dying. Some of the better perks fromthe Support skill tree include starting off each run with extra levels and being able to take damage foryour ally. However, now that I’ve made it to the end of the more difficult dungeon, I just seem to dieregardless of strategy, so perhaps I should have taken Defense instead.The wave defense mode is what you would expect. Hordes of slow-moving enemies try to attack yourally while you keep them alive. This is the first game mode in the tutorial, but the later modes includemore difficult bosses thrown in. Finally, Boss Rush feels like a reverse wave defense where a raid bossspawns first, followed by some weaker enemies.

So far, I can only comment on the initial boss as I haven’t been able to clear it and don’t know if thereare variations. The biggest problem is that periodically he will teleport you next to your healer and chainyou together to share damage. If you don’t move, one of you will end up dead very quickly, but if you dorun away there’s a good chance some of the smaller enemies will beat up your healer.Although I’m familiar with very similar tactics in MMORPGs, typically there are stronger, high-cooldownor crowd control abilities to use in these situations to keep everyone alive. Therefore, taking on anMMO raid boss with a single NPC and only basic rotation of skills leaves something to be desired.

A Horde of Bugs

While being able to churn out any half-decent game as a solo dev is an impressive feat, it’s difficult tooverlook some of the glaring flaws with Don’t Lose Aggro. If you can name a type of bug, it’s probably inthis game.

In my very first run, I got stuck in a wall that wasn’t a wall. Enemies will sometimes float away and othertimes they’ll explode off screen to return minutes later after you’ve already forgotten about them. Yourally can get stuck in weird positions, although they will usually correct themselves. This one might havebeen user error, but I couldn’t actually get the Ranger to do anything outside of manually casting itsactive ability.

Despite all of that, Don’t Lose Aggro is technically still in Early Access and does deliver a functional gamewith some entertainment value, especially at an incredibly low price point. It does, at least momentarily,scratch that tanking itch and provides a somewhat realistic scenario of what it’s like to tank in MMOraids; it even includes a healer who talks down to you. This is a good opportunity to support a solo devwith their vision and maybe get a few hours of tanking nostalgia, but if you’re looking for a full-fledgedgame it really does need more time in the oven. 

6.0 Okay Early Access Review

Pros

  • Captures nostalgic tanking in bite-sized sessions.
  • The basic tanking mechanics work pretty well.
  • Clear vision from the developer.

Cons

  • Lots of bugs.
  • Lacks content diversity.
  • Some enemies seem overpowered.

Early Access Reivew: This review is based on the Early Access launch of the game, and is not reflective of the game's status when it launches its 1.0 update.

To learn more about our approach to how we review Early Access and why we are applying scores to Early Access releases now, check out this post.

Nick Shively

Nick has been writing about games since 2011 and was formerly the editor-in-chief of another popular MMO website. While no longer involved full-time in the video game industry, he still likes to give the latest RPGs and MMORPGs a spin. When he's not playing video games, Nick likes to spend quality time with his family, battle it out in Warhammer 40k, and play Magic: The Gathering.



https://www.mmorpg.com/reviews/dont-lose-aggro-review-2000137897
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Final Fantasy XIV Finally Arrives on Nintendo Switch 2 in August 2026, New Evercold Expansion Arrives in January 2027
item.content:encoded:
After Naoki Yoshida told players to "please look forward to Final Fantasy XIV on Nintendo Switch," today, Square Enix not only revealed the sixth expansion for the game, Evercold, but also finally confirmed that the popular MMO will be making its way to the Nintendo Switch platform with a Nintendo Switch 2 version set for August 2026.

The news was revealed by Yoshida today during the Final Fantasy XIV Fan Festival 2026 in Anaheim, and you can check out the full keynote from the first day below if you want to see Yoshida take to the stage to announce the new expansion, followed by Square Enix president and chief executive officer Takashi Kiryu closing the presentation out by revealing the Nintendo Switch 2 version.

If you want to skip most of that and just jump to the Evercold trailer, you can check that trailer out below.

While it's exciting to know that players will finally be able to take one of the world's most popular MMOs on the go with the Nintendo Switch 2 version, there is an unfortunate caveat to the news of this new version.

Currently, Final Fantasy XIV requires one subscription to access it across all available platforms. But with the new Switch 2 version, even if you already pay for a subscription to play on PC, PS5, PS4, and Xbox Series, you'll have to pay for a separate subscription just to play on Nintendo Switch 2.

That means if you don't want to be paying for two subscriptions, you'll have to either choose between not playing on Switch 2, or making your Switch 2 your dedicated platform for playing Final Fantasy XIV. It's also worth noting that if you're already a console player, no matter which platform you choose, you already pay for two subscriptions to play Final Fantasy XIV: one for the game, and another for PS Plus/Xbox Game Pass.

The thing that makes it at least a little better is that current player subscribed to Final Fantasy XIV will get a 50% discount when buying a subscription to play on Nintendo Switch 2, and the fact that you'll be able to play it without a Nintendo Switch Online subscription.

For now, details on the new version are scarce, but it would be good if we can eventually reach a point where the second subscription isn't required.

Evercold, meanwhile will feature a level cap increase, two new jobs, new cities, new areas, new constructs to overcome, new trials, dungeons, PvP updates, a new ultimate raid, and a whole lot more. Similarly to the Switch 2 version, we'll have to wait for more details on that as well.

About the author: David has been writing about videogames, technology, and culture since 2020, with a focus on reporting daily news across multiple publications, including GameDaily.Biz, GameSkinny, and PlayStation Universe before joining Wccftech in 2025. David started contributing as Canada/US reporter for Wccftech's gaming section in 2025. Besides being up-to-date on the industry's movements, he loves interviewing developers, reviewing games, and writing intricate essays about the symbolism and layered meanings to be found in rich narratives as he's done for publications like GamesIndustry.Biz, LostInCult, and others. Outside of games he loves movies, music, theatre, his hometown, and his family, though not necessarily in that order.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/final-fantasy-xiv-nintendo-switch-2-august-2026-release-evercold-expansion/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: 12 Months And Dozens Of Awards Later You Now Have No Excuse Not To Play Clair Obscur, Which Is On Sale
item.content:encoded:
Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 got a small update for its one-year anniversary today, but what if you’re one of the people who has somehow managed to skip the game despite 12 months of acclaim and awards show sweeps? New haircuts for its cast of sad people mean nothing to you, right? Well, have you considered that it’s a great time to finally see what all the fuss is about? The game’s on sale on Steam for the next week.

The standard edition of Sandfall Interactive’s RPG is a bit cheaper on Steam until April 30, and will run you just $40. This includes all the free updates the game has received in the past year, so if you want a wonderful RPG that will hurt you so good emotionally and also give you a dynamic, challenging turn-based battle system to master, you can do it for a bit cheaper on PC.

If you’re already subscribed to Game Pass, you can just play it on Xbox at no additional cost, but if you’ve got a PC that can run it, $40 isn’t a bad deal. Though Clair Obscur can be completed in a brisk 30 hours or so, it has plenty of high-level endgame challenges to sweeten the deal, and the free updates Sandfall has been adding bring new trials for skilled players. That said, nothing is more difficult than the harrowing story of grief it tells, which always manages to keep you on your toes. Join Daredevil and Gustave actor Charlie Cox in the Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 Players club.

Once you’re done with it, you can join the rest of us in waiting to see what Sandfall Interactive does next.



https://kotaku.com/clair-obscur-expedition-33-sale-steam-anniversary-update-2000690558
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Rune Dice launches May 19 for PS5, Xbox Series, Switch, and PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Switch
  • Xbox Series

Tactical deckbuilder dated.

Tactical deckbuilder Rune Dice will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch, and PC via Steam on May 19, publisher Kwalee and developer Smart Raven Studio announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

Tactical deckbuilding in bite-sized sessions. Launch dice to create magical chain reactions. Choose from distinct hero classes with unique abilities. Build synergies between dice, relics, and runes to defeat challenging bosses.

Magical Dice Fusion

Launch dice across enchanted battlefields where realistic physics meets ancient magic. Matching dice fuse into a more powerful one that seeks its equals, triggering devastating chain combos.

Plan each throw carefully—every dice placement can trigger massive combos that wipe out entire enemy waves or set up tactical future plays. Master both precise aiming and strategic prediction to unleash the full power of mystical dice fusion.

Choose Your Hero Class

Pick from eight unique classes, each with special dice and magical abilities. Every class offers multiple heroes with different starting combinations, ensuring each adventure feels completely different. With endless replayability for each unique hero classes. Level up, complete quests, and unlock rewards.

Spread deadly poison through enemy ranks as a Rogue, unleash lightning storms as a Mage, or absorb massive damage as a heavily armoured Warrior. Each class opens up entirely new tactical approaches and synergies.

Face Epic Bosses

Every location culminates in challenging battles against bosses, each with unique abilities that will test your strategy.

Some bosses summon additional minions, others manipulate the dice field, and the most dangerous can completely change combat rules. Adapt your dice collection and tactics to overcome each distinct threat.

Build Your Perfect Deck

Craft a powerful combination of dice, relics, and runes that work together to define your playstyle.

Collect magical dice through combat victories and shop purchases, discover game-changing relics that provide permanent enhancements, and gather tactical runes for battlefield manipulation. Visit ancient altars where you can risk your dice for upgrades, or play it safe and build your arsenal gradually. The synergy between your dice attacks, relic bonuses, and rune abilities creates devastating combinations and endless strategic possibilities.

Key Features

  • Physics-based dice throwing with magical merge mechanics.
  • Eight hero classes with multiple characters each.
  • Epic boss and mini-boss encounters with unique abilities.
  • Diverse dice with special powers and abilities.
  • Powerful relics and consumable runes for synergies.
  • Procedural map generation with multiple encounter types.
  • Progression system with unlocks and achievements.

Watch a new trailer below.

Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/rune-dice-launches-may-19-for-ps5-xbox-series-switch-and-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: BrokenLore: DON’T PLAY announced for PS5, Xbox Series, and PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Follow a young hikikomori obsessed with video games and the need for recognition.

Serafini Productions has announced BrokenLore: DON’T PLAY, the next entry in its first-person psychological horror game series. It will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store. A release date was not announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via Serafini Productions:

About

BrokenLore: DON’T PLAY is a first-person psychological horror game that follows Hideo, a young hikikomori obsessed with video games and the need for recognition. When he receives a mysterious experimental console in the mail, he decides to play it, convinced it could change his life.

But each level brings increasingly unsettling consequences into the real world. His apartment begins to shift, time starts to distort, and something dark slowly emerges as the line between what he plays and what he lives begins to break down.

Across five levels that draw Hideo deeper into his obsession, BrokenLore: DON’T PLAY takes him on a disturbing psychological descent, exploring themes of alienation, the need for validation, and identity loss.

  • ❮
  • ❯

In this new installment, players will once again encounter familiar characters:

  • Hideo (this time as the protagonist)
  • Shinji (the protagonist of BrokenLore: DON’T WATCH)
  • Junko (the protagonist of BrokenLore: DON’T LIE)

Key Features

  • Face five increasingly disturbing levels of a mysterious experimental game, each with its own rules, mechanics, and hidden dangers.
  • Survive the consequences in the real world as your apartment slowly transforms after every session. What once felt familiar begins to change into something else entirely.
  • Immerse yourself in a first-person psychological horror experience featuring hyper-realistic visuals and relentless tension.
  • Uncover new fragments of the BrokenLore saga and piece together the mystery behind Elysium.

About the Series

BrokenLore: DON’T PLAY is part of the BrokenLore saga, a psychological horror anthology where each game stands on its own, yet hides fragments of a deeper truth. Through secrets, environments, and hidden details, every chapter reveals pieces of an unseen narrative, slowly forming the larger mystery known as Elysium.

Watch the announcement trailer below. View the first screenshots at the gallery.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/brokenlore-dont-play-announced-for-ps5-xbox-series-and-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Retro Soulslike action game Memoirium launches August 13
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Everything is but sand and stars.

Retro Soulslike action game Memoirium will launch for PC via Steam on August 13, publisher Outersloth and developer GoldenGratus announced.

“Memoirium is a testament to my love of games and video game narratives,” said GoldenGratus founder Paolo Villanueva in a press release. “No generative AI was used. This is a passion project that showcases my love of the soulslike genre and I can’t wait for everyone to experience this otherworldly title meant to help you ponder the existence of both the dreams you cherish and the dreams you fear.”

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

In Memoirium, everything is but sand and stars.

Traverse a chaotic and crumbling realm of dreams composed of liminal spaces. Adapt to the world’s odd rules as a Dreamer, and defy others like you to escape the strange and eclectic subconscious landscape.

Dream, defy, decorate.

Peculiar Locations

The realm of dreams throws all reason to the wayside. In one moment, you will find yourself in a simple 21st-century school, and in the next, your eyes will fall upon a castle held together by wax.

Masks and More

Most, if not every piece of equipment will alter your abilities and the entities you encounter. One set of armor influences how long negative statuses last, while a specific mask changes how enemies approach you in combat. Every item serves a purpose.

Consumed Now, but Not Forever

Put items to good use, as they will not disappear when consumed. Instead, items are fully replenished at certain points in your journey. Collecting the same item multiple times will upgrade it, but even equipment found in the realm of dreams has its limits.

Your Room

There also appears to be a mysterious location that you have control of in the dream world. Decorate this area of quiet reflection using goods found throughout your journey across the realm of dreams.

Key Features

  • A fully interconnected world with multiple paths.
  • Personal room to decorate and write in.
  • Eight unique dungeons with their own mobs, mini-bosses, and bosses.
  • 14 game-changing armors, masks, and off-hand items.
  • 25 Keepsakes (consumable items that are replenishable and provide passives).
  • 31 distinct weapons.
  • 67 powerful Lullabies (spells).

Watch a new trailer below.

Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/retro-soulslike-action-game-memoirium-launches-august-13
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Final Fantasy XIV is coming to Switch 2 in August!
item.content:encoded:
Square Enix had one final announcement during Final Fantasy XIV Fan Fest, revealing that they are bringing the huge MMORPG to Nintendo Switch 2.

This Switch 2 port of Final Fantasy XIV will launch in August, but this will be preceded by an early access period to test server stability.

This port comes with an…. “interesting” twist on the subscription business model. A separate Switch 2 subscription is required to play FF14 on Nintendo’s platform, while PC, PS5 and Xbox are able to share a subscription, however there are two concessions to this. For one, you won’t need to have a Nintendo Switch Online subscription to play FF14, and if you do hold an existing subscription for other platforms, the Switch 2 subscription will be 50% off.

That’s a real stinger for people that already play on other platforms and would like to dabble in portable play, but if you’re playing exclusively on Switch 2, then not needing Switch Online is nice.

All of this was at the end of a jam-packed opening showcase, which brought news of the next massive expansion, Evercold, which is launching in January 2027. Head here to find out more.



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/04/24/final-fantasy-xiv-is-coming-to-switch-2-in-august/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Square Enix unveils Final Fantasy XIV: Evercold – coming in January 2027
item.content:encoded:
Square Enix just blew the lid off at Final Fantasy XIV Fan Festival in Anaheim, announcing Evercold as the next expansion, set to debut in January 2027. But that wasn’t it, as they also detailed countless fan-pleasing features, improvements and more during a two hour keynote, including an Evangelion Crossover Alliance Raid, a Nintendo Switch 2 port, Shadowbringers being added to the Free Trial and more.

Evercold will be the start of the Godless Realms Saga story arc, with the teaser giving us a glimpse of what awaits the Warrior of Light.

A new alliance raid is also coming to Evercold, but this one has a major difference, as it’s also a crossover with the Evangelion anime, which is being created in collaboration with Khara Inc.

Digging deeper into the expansion, Evercold will bring a battle system overhaul with two distinct modes. Reborn will continue to be based on the current combat system and jobs, while Evolved will put more emphasis on job identity. New jobs will only be playable using Evolved mode, so consider this giving players notice on a new style of play going forward. They will demo this during Day 1 of the Fan Festival here.

Elsewhere, Evercold promises:

  • Two new jobs: tank and physical ranged DPS
  • Level cap increase from 100 to 110
  • New cities
  • Distinctive new areas
  • New gigantic constructs and other monstrous threats to overcome
  • New allies to encounter
  • New dungeons
  • New trials
  • A new raid series
  • A new Ultimate raid
  • PvP updates
  • Ongoing content updates, including new gear and crafting recipes, updates to the Duty Support System and more.

There will also be a new seasonal structure for rewards and character growth, an armoury update to accommodate those with multiple jobs, and more character customisation options.

On the path to Evercold and Patch 8.0, there’s still content coming through the rest of 2026 with version 7.5x. These include:

  • Patch 7.5 (releasing April 28) – New main scenario quests, new Trial, and Echoes of Vana’diel Part 3
  • Patch 7.51 (releasing June 2) – New Cosmic Exploration Star “Auxesia,” new Custom Deliveries: Tiisol Ja, and Dancing Mad (Ultimate)
  • Patch 7.55 (releasing July 28) – New updates to Inconceivably Further Hildibrand Adventures quests, the debut of Occult Crescent: North Horn, and new Phantom Weapon quests
  • Patch 7.56 (releasing Sept. 8) – New main scenario quests and introduction of beastmaster

And now should also be a great time to get into the game from scratch. The Free Trial edition of Final Fantasy XIV will be expanded on 28th April to include the third major expansion, Shadowbringers, including all content up to Patch 5.58. This means there’s hundreds of hours of the game to try out, with no limits on playtime, you’ll just be a good few years behind the current content.

And finally, while the writing is now on the wall for PlayStation 4 – they said that they anticipate the game will cross the technical size limit on PS4 around the time up version 8.3 in roughly two years time – a new game path is opening as Square Enix has now confirmed that Final Fantasy XIV is coming to Switch 2 in August. Head here for more details.

Source: press release



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/04/24/square-enix-unveils-final-fantasy-xiv-evercold-coming-in-january-2027/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: FFXIV Is Finally Getting A Switch 2 Port In August, With A Free Early Access--Though There Are Some Caveats
item.content:encoded:
The long-teased Switch 2 port of the critically-acclaimed MMORPG Final Fantasy 14 has finally been officially announced for an August release. 

At FFXIV’s ongoing FanFest in Anaheim, California, Square Enix CEO Takashi Kiryu shared the news in-person to conclude a long series of game-related announcements, including some of the basic details about this particular version. 

The subscription situation will be different for this specific port than the others. For all other platforms, one single subscription fee covers all other platforms, so long as each other platform’s version is purchased there (so you’d need to buy, say, Dawntrail twice for PC and Playstation, but only pay one subscription). 

FFXIV’s Switch 2 port will require not only its own purchase, as usual, but also its own separate FFXIV subscription fee. This comes with two slight pieces of good news, though—one being that you won’t need the Switch membership in order to play online (unlike Xbox, which requires the Games Pass). Also, those players who already have an active membership elsewhere, but may be wanting to play on the go, will get a half-off discount on the Switch version subscription. 

There’ll be a free to play “Early Access” soft launch lasting about one month for this version in order to ensure everything’s smooth, followed by the full initial release in August. 

FFXIV Director Naoki Yoshida has been talking about trying to port the Switch for a few months, with a firmer tease around the new year for fans. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/ffxiv-is-finally-getting-a-switch-2-port-in-august-with-a-free-early-access-though-there-are-some-caveats-2000137896
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Preview ESO's Night Market Factions, Rewards, and How Your Choice Impacts Your Experience
item.content:encoded:
We know when the Night Market event zone will open. We’ve met the faction leaders. Now, it’s time to learn about the factions those leaders represent (and rewards) in the latest Elder Scrolls Online preview.

When the Night Market opens on Wednesday, April 29th, there will be an introductory quest to get you started within the mysterious event zone. Once you complete that quest, you'll get to meet the three factions and eventually choose which one you want to align yourself with for the duration of the market. Each faction wants something, and all of them want to control wha the Night Market has to offer. When you lock in a choice, you’ll earn Favor for yourself and your chosen faction by completing activities within the zone.

The Glittering Goad is led by Tachien, a Dremora known for her political savvy and it’s not surprising that the faction sees power and wealth as linked. They’re ambitious and business minded, and consider themselves a meritocracy. You’ll want to have a gift for Tachien if you try to join.

Judging by their name, The Ruckus being a faction that prizes fighting prowess and physical strength is no surprise. They’re led by Kharajhal Moon-Eye, a Senche-raht and their ranks include both mortals and Daedra. This faction also offers protection at a price so it seems like they're open to deals and negotiation…but also force.

Finally, the Thousand Eyes is a faction of many of the "small and insignificant” and led by the Daedrat Molto the Whisperer. There are many of them all over, and underestimating them would be a mistake. Especially for their ruthlessness when they have a goal to complete.

When you choose a faction, your choice impacts the order in which you’ll be able to access quests within the zone, but not the content. Favor you earn adds up to rewards for you and a contribution to your faction’s overall total. Over time, you will grow your reputation, increase your standing and get access to the faction vendor, who sells stuff like item sets, Night Market Relic fragments, furnishings, and style pages. 

When the Night Market ends, one faction will win control, so that Favor is also counting for that goal. If your faction wins at the end, then you get bonus rewards like faction monster sets, banner furnishings, and gold.

And check out the full preview to learn some more and weigh your options. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/preview-esos-night-market-factions-rewards-and-how-your-choice-impacts-your-experience-2000137894
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Looks Like 'Mina The Hollower' Will Be Getting A New Release Date Soon
item.content:encoded:
The mouse has gone gold

Yacht Club Games has been pretty tight-lipped about its upcoming Game Boy-inspired, Zelda-style adventure Mina the Hollower, which is due to launch in spring 2026.

But the developer has revealed today that the game has finally gone Gold. So while we're approaching the end of that spring 2026 window, it sounds like we'll be getting a date very soon.

We would assume May, of course, but don't be surprised if it slips past that spring window, as Yacht Club Games has just "begun the submissions process with our first-party partners."

Still, we're eagerly anticipating to play this long-awaited follow-up to Shovel Knight, one of the best retro-inspired games we've ever played.

The beautiful pixel art action adventure is coming to both Switch 1 and Switch 2, with the latter supporting 120fps gameplay,, and last year, we got a limited-time demo on both consoles, which gave us a sweet early taste of the game.

The full release isn't far away, then. And we hope for the best, as Yacht Club revealed that this game could be a "make-or-break" situation for the team late last year.

Are you looking forward to Mina the Hollower? Do you think the game will be coming out soon? Let us know down below.

Alana has been with Nintendo Life since 2022, and while RPGs are her first love, Nintendo is a close second. She enjoys nothing more than overthinking battle strategies, characters, and stories. She also wishes she was a Sega air pirate.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/04/looks-like-mina-the-hollower-will-be-getting-a-new-release-date-soon
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Final Fantasy 14 Is Finally Coming To Switch 2 But There’s Apparently A Nintendo Tax
item.content:encoded:
Today’s Final Fantasy XIV Fan Fest keynote speech was almost absurdly packed with major announcements, including a release window and a title for its long-awaited sixth expansion, Evercold. At the end of what was already a really long keynote, however, the team behind the game also announced that FFXIV is finally headed to a Nintendo platform for the first time in its 13-year history…with a catch. 

During the keynote speech, Square Enix CEO Takashi Kiryu made a surprise appearance to deliver the very wild announcement that FFXIV is headed to the Nintendo Switch 2. The port is set to launch in August, with a month-long (and free!) early access test occurring right before.

Screams literally erupted the second the Switch 2 logo popped up on screen. The port has been rumored for a while now, and while a lot of people expected it to be announced today, it’s still pretty surprising that it actually happened. However, there’s an unfortunate caveat here. 

FFXIV is currently playable on PC, Mac, Xbox, and PlayStation with cross-platform subscriptions, although players still have to play for separate licenses on each platform. Like these other versions, the Switch 2 version will thankfully feature cross-progression. However, it’ll also require a separate subscription and a separate license (although that subscription will be half-price for players who have an active subscription on another platform). 

YoshiP really out here bringing back that E3 experience 😭 pic.twitter.com/H7z1feyM1J

— Cammy Dalamud (@Cammy_Dalamud) April 24, 2026

The FFXIV team said the pricing decision was made after “many months of discussion with Nintendo,” which I’m assuming is their way of gently saying “Nintendo was annoying and made us compromise.” On the other hand, though, a Nintendo Switch Online requirement isn’t required to play, which is kind of baffling. 

Director Yoshi-P brought out a Switch 2 to show off the game’s performance, and it looked pretty solid, although he basically just ran around the iconic Limsa Lominsa plaza for a quick second rather than showing off any real gameplay. Also, the Switch gameplay didn’t seem to be projected on the massive screen behind Yoshi-P like the rest of the presentation (and he commented that Nintendo “kind of freaked out a little” about having the port shown live on stage for some reason), so I’m a little worried. 

I am very intrigued to see how this massive game holds up on this tiny console, though, and I’m holding out hope. If Square Enix manages to pull it off, I could definitely see FFXIV appealing to a huge new audience, especially ahead of its massive Evercold expansion announced today. Unrelated but also important: shortly after announcing the expansion, Yoshi-P casually joked that the game’s newly improved character creator might receive a butt slider in the future. Man, I feel like I need to go on a walk or something after watching this insanely overwhelming presentation.



https://kotaku.com/final-fantasy-14-switch-2-subscription-ff14-2000690550
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Remake of the End of the Greatest RPG of All Time launches May 28
item.content:encoded:
The Remake of the End of the Greatest RPG of All Time will launch for PC via Steam on May 28, publisher Glootschke Games and developers Team Azule and Coin Drop Games announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

The Remake of the End of the Greatest RPG of All Time (“TRotEotGRPGoAT” for short) is the last hour of a lost (nonexistent) JRPG, “remade” in 3D pixel art complete with director’s commentary, the digital manual, and archival footage.

A Comedic and Bombastic Chapter

Players start at level 99, seeing the conclusion to story arcs they were never present for, heroic sacrifices by side characters they never knew existed, all while uncovering the meta-narrative of why they’ve never heard of this game, why they can only play the last hour, and why it’s being remade.

A Mystery at its Core

But beneath the shiny exterior lies something, can you uncover it?

… before it’s too late?

Watch a new trailer below.

Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/the-remake-of-the-end-of-the-greatest-rpg-of-all-time-launches-may-28
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
